firewall: TFPT 3/2 = relaxation-ladder step, NOT a two-oscillator ratio; mapping non-canonical; bare 3:2 match = coincidence-risk (GR parametric resonance also selects 3:2); only the ladder test discriminates; even a ladder hit would be [C]-tier; nothing here is [E]

combined.reading: 3/2 exact NOT rejected across the 4 consistent sources

counterexample.name: XTE J1859+226
counterexample.note: genuine published HFQPO pair far from 3:2 AND off the (3/2)^k ladder (log_1.5 ratio = 1.407, not an integer): 3:2 is NOT universal among BH HFQPO pairs

H1.honesty: consistency here is ambiguous BY CONSTRUCTION (coincidence-risk): GR parametric resonance also selects exactly 3:2, and H2 shows selection alone clusters ratios near 3/2

H2.observed: 4 of 5 published BH pairs within +-0.05 of 3/2 (J1859+226 breaks it)

deflator.reading: selection alone reproduces the observed 4-of-5 cluster in 18% of trials once the equal-rms detection window sits at the 3/2 crossing (empirically it does: Torok 2009) — the observed clustering does NOT beat the selection null, so it counts for nothing on its own

H2.honesty: this is a null that DEFLATES H1, not a test of TFPT: it quantifies how cheap the 3:2 cluster is under pure detection selection

prediction.tfpt_ladder: third QPO at nu_3 = (3/2) nu_u and NO integer harmonics
prediction.resonance_alternative: integer lines of nu_0 = nu_u/3 (e.g. 4 nu_0, nu_l/2)

per_source.name: GRO J1655-40
per_source.census: 450 Hz is the highest BH QPO frequency ever published (Strohmayer 2001, ApJ 552, L49; 4.5% rms, 13-27 keV). Belloni+2012 (MNRAS 426, 1701) searched 100-1000 Hz over 7108 archival PCA observations: 4 detections for this source, all in the 300-450 Hz dichotomy; nothing near 661.5 Hz and no per-frequency upper limit published there.

per_source.name: XTE J1550-564
per_source.census: Belloni+2012: 7 archive-wide detections, clustered near 180/280 Hz; nothing at 414 Hz. Varniere & Rodriguez 2018 (ApJ 865, 113) refit the stacked ~275 Hz group over 50-1000 Hz: 'no other peak is obvious' with 3 sigma upper limits ~1-1.8% rms for an additional HFQPO anywhere in the considered range — the closest thing to a published tooth limit (the known pair sits at 1.2-4.8% rms), but not targeted at 1.5 * nu_u.

per_source.name: GRS 1915+105
per_source.census: Belloni & Altamirano 2013 systematic RXTE search (MNRAS 432, 10): 51 detections, 49 between 58-72 Hz, two isolated peaks at ~134/143 Hz; the 168/113 pair itself only appears in stacked PDS (Remillard & McClintock 2006). No feature and no published limit at 252 Hz.

per_source.name: H1743-322
per_source.census: Remillard+2006 (ApJ 637, 1002) searched 50-2000 Hz in 111 intervals: one single->4sigma detection (239+-4 Hz); stacked detections 166/242 Hz only. No feature reported near 363 Hz, no per-frequency upper limit given.

anti_kernel_record.feature: marginal broad 92 Hz = 184/2 (integer subharmonic); Remillard+2002 modelled 92:184:276 as integer harmonics 1:2:3 of 92 Hz
simultaneous 34/68 Hz integer 1:2 pair (Belloni & Altamirano 2013); also the 41/69 Hz pair (Strohmayer 2001)
anti_kernel_record.weight: 4.2 sigma (different frequency regime from the 168/113 pair)

H3.stage1_finding: the teeth lie INSIDE the band RXTE PCA archive searches covered (100-1000 Hz), yet no published search targeted nu_3 = 1.5 nu_u and no per-frequency upper limits exist at the tooth frequencies; the only published third-frequency structures are INTEGER lines (92 = 184/2; 34/68), which favour the harmonic alternative
H3.next_stage: archival RXTE PCA event-data reanalysis specified in hypotheses/hfqpo_v1.yaml (targets, ObsID groups, stacking selections, detection/upper-limit rules); NOT run in this version
H3.honesty: even a future ladder hit would be [C]-tier: the ratio->ladder mapping is not canonical TFPT output, and the GR-resonance explanation remains the standing favorite

overall_reading: H1 consistent-but-ambiguous (chi2 p=0.78 across 4 sources, but J1859+226 breaks universality at 9.2 sigma); H2: anchored selection alone manufactures the 4-of-5 cluster in 18% of trials; H3 data_limited: no published x1.5 tooth search — the archival RXTE PCA design in the YAML is the decisive next stage
